channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: WFH CHADS REPORT IN — 53 replies — 212.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WFH CHADS REPORT IN

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-03-09 19:47 UTC

OP

Let's all laugh at the wagies who have to pay insane amounts for gas to get to work kek

Early replies (first 3)

61953886
>>61953855
Imagine spending half your days pay worth of money just to get to work and back lmao

61954053
>>61953855
antisemitic wfh antizog antimiga chads will inherit the earth

61954077
>>61953886
>half
You mean full for minimum wage chads, don’t forget about rent and bills too.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61958246
>>61957384
Even if it wasn't for the commute I couldn't go back into the office. Feels like a prison & I can't stand coworkers

61958290
>>61953855
Thank God for WFH. Happy Monday bros and when you're a WFH wagie, it actually is a happy Monday.

61958386
>>61957401
>>61958290
A toast to my WFH brothers, you wearing PJs this morning too?

61958439
Posting from in bed because I /madeit/ thanks to biz :3

61958532
>>61953855
Another day, another opportunity to work 0 hours and put 8 on my timesheet.
What do you guys do? I'm a consultant for a niche eng field. 3rd party contractor (hence timesheets to record hours per project).

61958544
>>61958246
I actually liked my coworkers and shootin the shit at the office, but since March of 2020 I've been 100% WFH and prefer this 10x more. Fuck driving in traffic.

61958574
>>61958386
I was wearing my standard comfy pants but have now slipped into my socially acceptable gear (jeans) for my daily walk with my dog. Cheers brother, enjoy this fine day.

61958588
>>61953855
WFH is true king status. Sometimes I fuck my wife on the clock. I've had her under my desk sucking my dick while on a teams meeting. So good.

61958609
>>61954954
Job market is brutally bad right now. That makes us folks who have good WFH jobs even bigger Chads. And yea, fake jobs out the ass, AI recruiters, AI interviews, fake shit all over.

61958767
i work from home everyday and i get paid well.
problem is that my apartment has become my prison. i have become more agoraphobic than over and depression is creeping in. my portfolio is doing good though.

61958794
>>61958767
Go take a walk for 15 minutes whenever you feel that. Always cures it for me

61959001
>>61953855
What field do you faggots work in where wfh jobs fall from the sky like rain?
And don't fucking say tech cuz every tech role near me is fully onsite

61959006
>>61958588
I remember fucking my gf on my lunchbreak once lol it was a good memory

61959021
>>61959001
If you have the skills, any job will allow you to work from home that doesn't have a specialized worksite (Lawyer::Courtroom, Soldier::Iran, Tradesman::Jobsite)

61959146
What WfH jobs are there where you basically don't do shit? Or if you have work, no one will know if it's completed or not

61959227
>>61953855
At the end of the day, we're still wagies. I want to die, I hate this. I don't want to work!
t. have 2 WFH jobs at once, still not enough, still do doordash after work for some extra cash. Made $115k in 2025

61959302
>>61959227
>still not enough
You might be in a similar boat as me, perhaps slightly financially irresponsible? I work one job for the same amount, but didn't listen and lived above my means with the nice amount of money. Start living below your means, spend less, pay off the debt and you should be able to breathe. Making over 100k we shouldn't be suffering
>as if I'm going to listen to this as well kek

61959314
>>61958588
This is literally how I got my now my wife pregnant. Be careful. I was having too much careless sex.

61959328
>>61959146
You need to actually work. WFH isn’t magic free money

61959331
>>61959302
I can definitely admit to spending a bit too much but the main problem is that I have a family. Wife and now 2 kids (my son was born in January). Wife does not work, so I pay all the bills. I was also super irresponsible before I got into this position so I had a lot of debt and that's pretty much where most of the extra money is going. I have 5k credit card debt left to pay off, slowly but surely I'm almost there. But things would be moving faster if I either made more money, or if my wife worked as well.
I am literally getting ready to apply for a third WFH job just to try and get myself over that threshold so I can start to really save and get out of debt faster. My biggest spending is on food, I love to eat out, it's my only joy in this horrible existence beyond my kids.

61959344
>>61953855
Jokes on you I drive an electric car.

61959354
>>61959328
>You need to actually work
Ok what WfH jobs require the least effort

61959370
>>61959354
That really depends on the company anon. The easiest is any kind of support desk position, but depending on the company, you could be super busy. Usually, the ones that keep you barely busy will have you do auxiliary shit to make up for your lack of busyness, so you're always going to have some level of work you don't wanna do.

I have 2 WFH jobs because one of my jobs is a lot less involved, they don't monitor you, barely on the phone (mostly emails) but technically I should be doing more (writing articles/making tutorial videos) and I'm just lazy and/or distracted by other job which needs you to be infront of the laptop almost the entire time (phones, emails and chat), hard to get away from it, and they track the shit out of your metrics. I hate it, but the second one is what gives me health insurance and other benefits.

61959379
>>61959331
literally me except no family, damn I'm sad

>>61959354
Being a leader/manager in something. I work in finance and am a low level manager. I started as a nobody on the phones and worked my way to that. I work at home half the time, but I'm gaming 95% of the time when I'm at home. When I'm in a meeting I make sure to not be the presenter that way I can just talk/listen while playing games. The saddest part is I'm still more productive than most people. Us gaming while working is the equivalent of women walking around with StarBucks and talking about nothing all day at work... jk we still do more than they ever would

61959385
>>61953855
AI is going to make everyone return to office.

61959429
>>61959385
???

61959568
>>61958767
go do the work at coffeeshop meme. parks are nice too. my favorite is work from ski lodge

61959577
Well. My wife still goes into jewish daycare every day, so we are still spending money on gas.

61959674
>>61954808
Also accountant here. How easy was it to get a wfh position? What industry? What state?

61959787
>>61954808
Also accountant here. How easy was it to get a wfh position? What industry? What state?

item.title: >last night oil futures were nearing 120 >reports of G7 will be releasing SPR >oil drops under 100 >later find out that… — 30 replies — 119.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
>last night oil futures were nearing 120 >reports of G7 will be releasing SPR >oil drops under 100 >later find out that…

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-03-09 19:35 UTC

OP

>last night oil futures were nearing 120
>reports of G7 will be releasing SPR
>oil drops under 100
>later find out that they never planned on doing it


So they're just releasing fake news to make people panic sell?

Early replies (first 3)

61958573
>>61958562
What's petrol gonna cost per liter in Europe when oil is $250 a barrel?

61958593
>>61958562
Yes and no. The whole war is a psyop so that they can sell the oil reserves/venezuala raid oil at an insane markup before prices return to a more neutral stance

61958611
>>61958573
2 dollar per liter or 13 per gallon. US would probably be half but idk if modern society can operate with those costs? And if Iranians are destroying oil infrastructure, Arab states shutting down oil production which takes months to restart and ports are being bombed it might take long time for natural flow to restart. But idk why they lied about it though

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61958627
>>61958593
>l reserves/venezuala raid oil


Venezuela oil infrastructure is decrepit and can barely hit 1 million while Hormuz is 20 million per day. And that million will need investments and years to even match UAEs production per day.

61958703
>>61958627
The US has appropriated 80 million barrels of oil from venezuala since the new year. A clean 4 billion in additional value since the Iran conflict started, of course headed straight for private hands. We've only spent 100 billion in taxpayer money on Iran so far, a very efficient laundering ratio

61958725
>>61958703
>The US has appropriated 80 million barrels of oil from venezuala since the new year. A clean 4 billion in additional value since the Iran conflict started, of course headed straight for private hands.


And Hormuz makes 20 million barrels a day. So within this week we lost 160 millions of barrels heading into global oil market. Can Venezuela fulfill that gap for rest of global market? India , east Asia , South America and Europe? At best it can help stave off few cents off gallon in US not replace Hormuz.

61958760
>>61958562
what's the purpose of strategic reserves when you do not use them in a scenario like this? do they think russia will invade them right after they burned through them or something?

61958770
>>61958725
Fill the gap? I don't think you're reading what I'm saying. The US has reserve oil that it (read: private interests) wants to sell at a premium, and it has a convenient piñata that it can hit to make that happen in Iran. The oil price has been rock bottom for an extremely long time. The health of the global economy or whatever has not been, is not, and never will be the priority of the US government

61958797
>>61958562
Buy the dip

61958837
>>61958770
>The US has reserve oil that it (read: private interests) wants to sell at a premium, and it has a convenient piñata that it can hit to make that happen in Iran. The oil price has been rock bottom for an extremely long time. The health of the global economy or whatever has not been, is not, and never will be the priority of the US government


>East Asia hit by energy crisis
>electronic manufacturing needed for mass data center build up stalls or becomes increasingly expensive
>AI companies either get in more debt or halt production

So biggest growth sector so far has been tech and you're telling me they're willing to throw it all away for oil barons in Texas to sell expensive oil that will have little to no demand. Not only is oil scarcity bad enough Qatar makes helium used in semiconductor manufacturing so what will Taiwan use then to keep up with big demand for next gen chips.

61958856
>>61958562
That fake news cost me over 10 grand.

61958857
>>61958573
Some 'expert' here in Australia was saying that every $1USD per barrel that crude goes up we see an increase of $0.1AUD per litre at the pump.
Fuel was around $1.70 per litre before the war and is now between $2 and $2.20 so seems to be holding true so far, but if oil reached $250 a barrel I'm sure this math would break down. But it would be $3.50AUD per litre at an absolute minimum.
Fuel in Europe is usually more expensive than Australia, maybe due to taxes you guys impose or something. But $3.50AUD is €2.13 so don't expect to pay less than that if oil goes to $250.

61958935
>>61958837
Frankly, yes. If the US had actual planning capability the oil price would not have spiked, such is the privelege of being the world reserve currency. Israel is their greatest ally, and they can slide in some backdoor deals while they're executing their god-given mission to bomb iran. I'm not saying this is a good idea or anything, but this is what you do when you're a billionaire who wants more billions

61959019
>>61958935
Most of US economic growth is in AI for past few years. Most of these AI companies are owned and run by Jews like Ellison and Sam Altman. If they truly believed these things could become AGI and be smarter than any human combined they wouldn't have attacked Iran while risking the biggest private sector build up ever seen in US history. Plus Arab money is fueling this industry as well so not only are they hurting East Asian companies who make chips and parts needed but capital investment that keeps it going. Now we might see billions lost , companies default and bubble might pop if this goes on any longer.

61959081
>>61959019
What does that have to do with trump's pocketbook? Given the track record, he (using a proxy) almost certainly shorted the shit out of whatever dips on rising oil prices. In the end this whole thing is a stunt, it'll be back to normal in a week. Remember, nothing ever happens. This is all a game being played with peoples' lives

61959087
>>61959019
If Israel inadvertently kills the AI industry I will forgive them for everything, even 9/11.

61959119
>>61959081
Iran will just roll over after their leader was killed , oil refinery destroyed and navy obliterated? The new leader had his whole family wiped out
>>61959087
There's secular diaspora Jews and religious Jews who want messiah.

61959142
>>61958856
Has /biz/ never bought the top?

61959255
>>61959119
The new leader will be compelled into compliance under threat of continuing decapitations of leadership, they understand that there is only one way to stop the hemorrhaging. The only thing which would cause the war to go long is if israel demands land such as it did with palestine

61959275
>>61959255
Americattle are so stupid. Look up how many leaders in Afghanistan the usa killed and it accomplished nothing.

61959347
>>61959275
I'm not american, and the goal in afghanistan was for the US to implement a mutated puppet government in constrast to Iran now where the goal is ostensibly just to kill a bunch of middle-easterners, bomb some sites, and leave

61959420
I'm impressed, they managed to suppress a +30% pump into a +3% one. Just on fake news.
Now, really, I'm deeply impressed. Either by how they made it happen, or by how the fucktarded market believes that shit.

61959433
>>61959087
You're a cheap whore, like nigger do you HEAR yourself? "If they jews stop molesting and violating me as hard then I'll get stockholm syndrome and forgive all the other heinous shit they've done that has caused the deaths of countless innocent people".

The AI is just part of a bigger problem.

61959473
>>61959420
market is essentially swayed by news even it's fake

61959484
BUY USO BEFORE IT'S TOO LATE GOYS

61959498
>>61959484
I put in $7,500 on Friday. Let's ride.

61959506
>>61959142
No I still made almost $9k this morning after selling but I would have been up around $20k on my options if it stayed around 20% up for the day.

61959570
>>61959420
>betting against the market maker (US government) which can invent as many dollars as it needs to in order to move derivatives where they need to be to achieve geopolitical goals
Until there are actual resource shortages, the US will not allow commodities to experience price discovery that would cause its interests to be undermined. Anyone making bets that the GM is going to lose because of “the rules” is hopelessly retarded. Markets are not governed by any rules like physical systems and once you stop seeing them as anything other than a RP scenario where the GM decides where they will go, you will continue losing money betting that the GM has to follow the same rules as players in the system. They literally announced they will be manipulating derivatives to keep the price contained and you think they’re just going to throw away global hegemony because of a line on a screen? Lol, lmao even.

61959625
>>61958593
>the people in power can't be incompetent because that's too scary, so whenever they do something retarded i pretend that it's part of a genius secret plan that only i know about

61959694
>>61959625
I would never suggest they were competent

item.title: I put roughly $400k on USO puts at strike price $90 expiring march 27th. — 29 replies — 116.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I put roughly $400k on USO puts at strike price $90 expiring march 27th.

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-03-09 19:50 UTC

OP

I put roughly $400k on USO puts at strike price $90 expiring march 27th.

I did xanax and blacked out and placed the trades and fell asleep. it's my entire account and my net liquidity is like $13k or something.

so hopefully oil go down a lil bit or else im gonna have a heart attack on monday. i havent eaten and I still have no appetite. i cant even post pictures of the positions (im already down a bit on them)

I wish I was kidding, I cant feel my face and I start screaming every now and then. I already had the police called on me because the neighbors got worried and I explained it to the cops and they just went quiet and wished me luck.

I tried calling the exchange but there was nothing they could do.

Early replies (first 3)

61950275
Are you retarded? Rhetorical question. Sell them at open and take the idiot tax

61950614
>I explained it to the cops and they just went quiet and wished me luck

Cops don’t know what options are.

61950653
>>61950166
This is a larp until you post postions

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61950671
its gongo up

61950686
>>61950166
I have USO $150 puts expiring 12/18. But I only put $1700 lol. Starting to think it's a bad idea, oil fields are on fire so oil might pop off on Monday. Might be a good time to buy puts on Monday though, can't imagine any news being worst than oil fields on fire in Iran

Also wtf is up with the captcha. They are hard

61952836
wow i cant breathe

61952840
For once in my stupid fucking life I did not sell too early

61953162
fuck my life

61953244
This kind of line would be called an asymptote, correct?

61953267
>>61950166
you're totally fucked

61953317
>>61950166
Doing retarded drugs when trading?
You fucking deserve it.

61953326
>>61950166
>>61952836
>>61953162
I'm starting to understand why financial regulations for retail exist. Some people will voluntarily commit financial suicide if they're allowed to.

61953440
>>61950614
he explained it to them!

61953445
>>61950166
Why in the everloving fuck would you think oil would go down? You're such a fucking retard. You deserve to be broke.

61953510
it's so fucking over

61953511
>>61950166
>I explained it to the cops and they just went quiet and wished me luck.
don't even care if it's a larp, my laugh was genuine

61953538
>betting oil prices will go DOWN in the middle of a war where one of the actor's primary method of damage is economic warfare, SPECIFICALLY RAISING OIL PRICES FOR LONG PERIODS OF TIME
you deserve to lose the money

61953662
>>61950166
nah you'll be fine

61956460
Life hack: if you do enough xanax time reverses and everything you did gets reset. Worth a shot OP

61956999
>options on xanax
could be a movie really

61957012
>>61950614
lol yes they do nowadays

61957022
also xanax will ruin your life please stob and at least sell half

61959704
>>61950166
so op whats up ?

61959720
>>61959704
lost $100k

61959746
>>61959720
at least you felt like a trader for 48 hours

61959750
>>61959720
fak but at least you dont have to kys

61959772
>>61959746
>>61959750
i have a drug problem and im gonna get help

61959784
>>61959720
USO is at $103 how are you down 100k?

61959801
>>61959784
im not going to explain how options work with you bud. and im sure you can understand my situation, do your own research. im done with this thread.

hint: volatility

item.title: WEAT - the play everyone is missing — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WEAT - the play everyone is missing

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-09 19:40 UTC

OP

This is potentially the most consequential second-order effect from the oil crisis and almost nobody in retail trading is talking about it yet.
Oil and natural gas aren't just fuel — they're the foundational feedstock for nitrogen fertilizer.
The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia, which becomes urea, ammonium nitrate, and other fertilizers.
Roughly 2-3% of global energy consumption goes to fertilizer production alone. When natural gas prices doubled after Russia invaded Ukraine, fertilizer prices tripled and we got a global food price crisis.

Now consider: European natural gas has nearly doubled already in one week. Qatar - one of the world's largest LNG exporters - declared force majeure. 20% of global LNG transits Hormuz. Europe rebuilt its entire energy security strategy around LNG after cutting off Russian gas.

The third-order effect: We're entering spring planting season in the Northern Hemisphere
RIGHT NOW. Farmers make input decisions in March-April. If fertilizer prices spike 50-100% (which is beginning), farmers either plant less, use less fertilizer (lower yields), or absorb costs they can't recover. The food price inflation from this shows up in August-October harvests -
5-6 months from now.

They can release strategic oil reserves. There are no strategic fertilizer reserves. Food prices are about to be fucked, and as you can see from the chart - if you get in early, you can profit. Let retards from Reddit chase oil prices around. Go ahead and wait for them at the finish line.

You’re welcome fuckfaces

Early replies (first 3)

61957829
>>61957787
>This is potentially the most consequential second-orde
stopped reading here. i hate faggots who try to sound smart. judging by your pic, you think wheat is going to pump? youre a fucking retard.

61957846
Ukraine was also a producer of wheat thobeit

61957902
>>61957829
I’m also right. I’m sorry you get all your trading ideas from mouth breathing retards (HURR DURR LETS LONG OIL. HURR DURR LETS BUY HIMS. Wut about muh chainlink!).

Sometimes you might want to try listening to well thought out theories and intelligence for a change. Yes you fucking retard, wheat is going to asplode. But I actually prefer if you stay out; I’d rather someone like you not profit a dime off it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61957928
>>61957846
Ukraine reduced the wheat supply. Iran is now reducing the ability to grow more wheat to replace it. Those aren’t the same thing, anon.

The 2022 Ukraine shock alone sent WEAT from $8 to $40+. That was one disruption to one input (supply of wheat itself). Now you have the original Ukraine disruption still ongoing PLUS a simultaneous disruption to the inputs needed to grow wheat everywhere else (fertilizer). It’s a compounding problem, not a competing one.

61957973
>>61957787
Or they just mine all needed fertilizers as usual.

61957990
>>61957902
You are retarded manchild. Midwit if you will.
You will never be good trader or investor.

61957997
>>61957787
All of the price action in markets now is paper pricing, retail just jumps into whatever name they think will go up, they pump it up and crash it. They don't consider 'second order effects', if it's not something they can figure out with two brain cells then they don't jump in. That or you need a jewtuber to shill it.

61958020
>>61957997
Not really tough. Things go up and down all the time, while no one cares. It's just that OP's kind retards don't have a glue what they are looking for.

61958148
>>61957829
This, and its a general phenomena. Everyone today hates the nigger trying to sound like an authoritative source and is incentivized to either ignore the sales pitch or to do the exact opposite

61958421
>>61957973
Nitrogen fertilizer is synthesized from natural gas, not mined. 33% of global fertilizer trade transits Hormuz. QatarEnergy’s urea plant is offline. Urea up 30% in a week. Spring planting is happening now. But sure, they’ll just mine it.

You don’t mine nitrogen fertilizer. The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia, which becomes urea. It’s an industrial chemical process that requires massive quantities of natural gas as feedstock. The gas comes from Qatar (shut down), the Gulf states (can’t ship through Hormuz), and is processed at plants in the Middle East (several offline). You can’t just dig urea out of the ground with a pickaxe.
Phosphate and potash are mined, yes; but phosphate requires sulphur to process, and 45% of global sulphur exports transit Hormuz. So even the stuff you can mine still needs inputs that are stuck in the Persian Gulf right now.
>>61957997
That’s literally my thesis; everyone is piling into oil while the fertilizer-to-food chain is the second-order play that hasn’t been fully priced.

61959325
>>61957787
Nigga I bought 150x weat Jan 27 calls months ago

61959715
>>61957902
>I’m
your op is literal ai slop nigger kys

61959724
real chads are always long grain wood stone gold
and ore. its called being an RTS realpolitik knower

i guess since you have dubs we follow your command

61959338

Why would it be, it's fully traceable. XMR is though

are you a pedo?

item.title: What's the best way to generate yield and borrow against the asset? — 4 replies — 11.2/hr

item.title: AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHHHHH — 104 replies — 98.6/hr

Open thread • Replies: 104 • Updated: 2026-03-09 18:47 UTC

OP

FUCKING OIL IS $104!!!!!!!!!
+14%%%% AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHH

Early replies (first 3)

61953190
this man is on suicide watch >>61950166

61953192

>how the fuck is it still below $200?

61953223

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61956488
>>61956478
WOW CRAMER ACTUALLY FUCKING JINXED THE ENTIRE THING. FUCK HIM FUCK JIM FUCK JIM

61956494

61956501
>>61956494
They dont deal in the real world, anon

61956502
>>61956442

>>61956488

61956504

61956656

61957087
>>61956566

It's like how people thought WW1 was going to last a couple months because there hadn't been a major war in Europe for a hundred years aside from the Prussians sealclubbing the Austrians and French when they were weak.

61957147

61957235

refusing to take the L is not the same as winning

61957296
>>61957229
>America will be fine for the most part its us in Europe and Koreans/japanese
i say this as a europoor but i think we have a better shot than the US, but only really because i think the US is so incredibly badly mismanaged right now. biden admin could have probably done the same broad strokes as trump but accomplished them much more competently.

u guys will probably hate the comparison but it looks like ww2 pre-war german/italian shenanigans except now it's US that's impossible to work with due to retarded leadership. if you're a random country like canada or whatever, you're probably more likely to want to integrate more with EU than the US or china at this point. it's clear US has given up on soft power and is trying to just strong-arm countries directly (which doesn't really work if you're trying to do it to everyone at once)

>unless everyone plans a coordinated attack on the US dollar
pretty sure china is basically already trying to by not buying bonds anymore but idk what exactly it would take (getting rid of the petro dollar?)

>>61954970

61957310

>>61954140

Buckle up, because this is only gonna get worse.

item.title: There are now more tech workers going from the US to Europe than the other way around — 39 replies — 52.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are now more tech workers going from the US to Europe than the other way around

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-03-09 19:05 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61952823
>>61952797
is this another one of those tricks where India becomes Southern Asian becomes Eastern Europe?

61952844
>>61952797
Ok, but why are both decreasing?

61952879
>>61952844
it looks to me that there are less Tech workers in general, the ones going back to Europe probably couldn't get a job in the US

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61955722
>Sending in all the saars to Europe
Pretty based tee bee haych. We should help them with a one-way ticket to the whitest europe countries left remaining

61955732
>>61952797
>better women
>better food
>better culture
yeah

61956664
>>61952901
Europeans save 12-15% of their income (in West Europe up to 20%). Americans ~5%.

61956667
America is being sacrificed. Get out while u can.

61956668
>>61952881
>europe is currently in a tech boom
…

61956671
Unconquered.

61956746
>>61952797
that's h1b people getting kicked out of US
>>61956671
every cunt in EU was conquered like 50 times in history

61956786
>>61956746
I mean europe as a whole.

61956789
>>61956668
The number of private tech start ups valued above $1bn in Europe has gone up from ~15 to ~300 in about 10 years time.

61956794
>>61956786
Name 1 continent that was ever conquered as a whole. The fuck kind of retarded goalpost is that

61956838
>>61952797
The amount of mutt coping in this thread is absolutely hilarious. These brown subhumans never learn.

61956843
>>61956838
It's really not Americans, anon. It's majority thirdies (arabs, russians).
Probably some mutts as well, but they are mostly asleep right now.

61956849
>>61956794
The idea is that the goal of europeans is to not be conquered by foreign powers right now, retard.

61956857
>>61956664
This is cultural. Americans are encouraged to spend 115% of their income while European culture values security thru saving.
>>61952797
I am a U.S. tech worker in Europe. It isn't bad if you already have money from America to buy a house in Europe. I get literally over 2 months off per year between all the PTO and holidays. Pay is about 1/3 what I made in America but it's WFH at least and no niggers at all near me.

61956859
>>61956857
>no niggers at all near me
Don't say that in 4chan. Anons will call you a liar and tell Europe is majority African.

61956861
>>61956668
Spotify
Revolut
Various other fintech companies
Video game companies are doing well (except ubisoft)

61956865
Oh yea the other big deal in European tech is the desire to move away from U.S. based tech.
This will drive a huge boom in E.U. tech and coding jobs for 15+ years.
Americans really underestimate how petty and spiteful Europeans are. The people I work with don't even want to use U.S. clouds or Windows at all.

61956870
>>61956865
Any ways you can think to profit off of Linux? I feel it is finally reaching normal people, irregardless of Europeans

61956885
>>61952797
>Europoor cope.
Just ask an AI what the economic outlook is for the next 10 years for the US. Then for any Europoor country.

61956888
>>61956870

Charge companies $$$ to install open source systems and maintain them.
Remember, you are charging them for the labor and not the software itself.

61956914
>>61956870
>>61956888

So yea, but you need to speak German. But in general there is an uptick in E.U. demand for Linux experts...

61959430

61959479

61959509

item.title: The US has 10 days — 170 replies — 42.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US has 10 days

Open thread • Replies: 170 • Updated: 2026-03-09 15:47 UTC

OP

The US has 10 days to clear the strait of Hormuz or global markets will crash (not a hyperbole)

At first, markets brushed off the conflict as another 12 day war. However, realization is setting in that Iran is actually capable of deterring passage through the Hormuz strait by 90%.

The problem is that every day that goes on is the equivalent of shutting off 10+ billion barrels of oil every single day. As storage becomes full, producers are forced to shut in production, which takes 2 weeks to restart. Thankfully, most of the world is fine for 1-2 weeks without gulf oil. After that, problems begin.

Oil will reprice a geopolitical premium and $100 is LOW for the magnitude of production shutdown.

What happens: bond yields will roar to new highs as a huge wave of inflation begins. All rate cut probabilities get removed, and you will see the traditional end of the business cycle, high oil prices will turn growth into recession for most economies. None of this is priced in equities. And the longer it goes on, the higher likelihood of it happening.

How to end it? Trump has fallen for a Trump. There is no way to clear irans fast mountain ranges littered with AD and missile silos, thus tankers will keep getting shot in the Hormuz strait. The only way is to put troops on the ground.

Don’t really care if you think I’m a permabear or a doomer, I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in. They don’t understand that this is an existential war for Iran, it’s not some “geopolitical dip”.

Pic is Asian jet fuel prices

Early replies (first 3)

61938343
>>61938341
Slurp XOP? Wut do

61938344
So should I sell my TLT?

61938345
>>61938341
>I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in
Trump spent all of last year insider trading and TACOing so he's not trustworthy anymore

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61956110
>>61938402
Nah you can launch shaheads from the back of a ute, I've seen it done
Also, USVs do not require any launch platform at all and have proven devastatingly effective in the black sea - though with that said I have yet to see Iran or its houthi proxies deploy those in any serious number

61956116
>>61938341
just got into trading, is it supposed to be doing that?

61956309
>>61956116
yes. it's normal.

61956552
>>61956041
>meanwhile in Iran

61956572
>>61938341
two more weeks

61956577
how much time would you wager a release of circa 400 million barrels from the G7 would buy?

61956580
>>61956577
Consumption is ~100m/day desu

61956581
>>61956577
If it is just meant to replace average Hormuz traffic (20 million barrels a day), that's 20 days?

61956822

>>61954112
It'll probably be on it anyways

61957005

61957018
>>61938341

back to your rice farm buddy.

61957024
>>61957005

why can't any of you speak english?

where do they find you illiterate faggots?

61957027
>>61957024
at ur mom

61957036
>>61957027
bloody benchod your sister you basterd

61957048
>>61957018

You don’t know what a hyperbole is? You drop out of high school?

61957128
>>61957036
I stand redeemed.

61957134
>>61957048
It's
>hyperbole
not
>A hyperbole

you say "a hyperbole" and everyone knows you're from india

61957139
>>61956041

61957381
>>61942837
>il price going up means US gov will have more money to balance the budget
the us gov does not get anything from oil prices going up kek. Slightly higher taxs from oil companies but not in any siginificant amount.

61957411
>>61957134

I’m not from India I literally have a masters degree from an Ivy League. I don’t give a fuck what some loser thinks. Post your longs or get out of my thread. We already know you got burned on your weekend CL shorts

61957592
Frens, leave stonks.
Switch to bonds real quick.

61957796
>>61938345
As other anon said, wholy retard.
>Trump made US oil independent during first term, and US is top 3 oil producing country now.
>Oil price going up means US gov will have more money to balance the budget.

61957822
>>61938445
>one of the underlying objectives was to tank the economies of EU
EU economies are tanking and dying by themselves.
EU is collapsing, Germany, the largest economic EU powerhouse is having mass layoffs.

>. One way to onshore manufacturing and heavy industry is to be one of the few places with relatively cheap energy
Correct.
>WW3 scitzos
were wrong .
Why do we need ww3, if you want to restart manufacturing? They don't need a war.

61957823
>>61938341
Fake news! Democrat Hoax! Not confirmed by CENTCOM, plus it looks like AI. Be aware of malinformation.
MAGA!
Now is a good time to buy ETH

61957832
>>61938425
IF you don't understand geo politics, this doesn't mean Trump and his team don't.
It just means YOU don't understand it.

61957835
>>61957796
This anon gets it

61957837
>>61957832
This! MAGA!!

61957854
>>61938341
Rolling that this war goes on for years months and the global economy shits itself.

61958442
>>61957018
>a hyperbole
You're retarded. Hyperbole is a noun and you are too used to retarded internet speak that uses it as an adjective (which is hyperbolic).

Furthermore, dismissing a message because of grammatical or linguistic issues you find in the message is retarded. Even if "hyperbole" were an adjective, were you really unable to understand the meaning of his message? Language is about transacting messages/meaning. If the message was delivered, that is all that matters.

t. engineer working on semantic compression method for agent-to-agent communication

61958506
need some stock picks for slurpage. if the market crashes

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 25 replies — 25.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-03-09 18:51 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

item.title: Literally MFW I am just gonna buy the dip, wait, and reap all the rewards It's called investing kid, read up on it — 12 replies — 13.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Literally MFW I am just gonna buy the dip, wait, and reap all the rewards It's called investing kid, read up on it

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-03-09 18:55 UTC

OP

Literally MFW I am just gonna buy the dip, wait, and reap all the rewards
It's called investing kid, read up on it

Early replies (first 3)

61955022
>>61954966
I just DCA regardless of what's going on.

61955047
>>61955022
not bad either, but you are missing potential upside
that being said, waiting forever for a dip and never buying in is also retarded

61955063
>>61955047
I also have cash for a lump-sum, but I only will do that if the market crashes more than 20%.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61955098
No, that's trading if you are trying to time it. Investing is just DCA at payday, like the gentleman above said.

61955107
>>61955047
>>61955063
what would you buy? i just opened an IRA account i'm trying to learn everything

61955328
>>61955107
In my taxable account I only buy index funds covering large and mid cap companies with very low turnovers and very low fees.

My Roth IRA is more specialized and is where I'll have some sector focused funds or some small cap stocks.

61955378
>>61955098
It’s investing just with a certain timing

61956412
Time in the markets beats timing the markets. Timing the markets simply activates the same monkey brain part that gambling does so people latch onto it thinking "they'll be different and succeed"

61956444
https://youtu.be/hBldBiZnafY

61959342
Uhhhhh
Where was the dip

61959489
ehm, where does one invest on? asking for a fren here

61959523
>>61959489
You already know fren...

>the protest is violently suppressed
>the glowie cries about how violently it was suppressed while acting innocent
At this point you're not even falling for a lie because they openly talk about all of this. You're just choosing to believe lies because they feel good

item.title: ECON EXPERTS GET IN HERE — 17 replies — 7.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
ECON EXPERTS GET IN HERE

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-03-09 17:39 UTC

OP

I see so much shilling from both sides of the aisle right now about how the economy will hold up, both in the United States and also around the world.
So, what is it? Will the global economy collapse? Will the United States economy collapse?
What can we expect from oil prices?
I was doom-posting when I saw the oil futures hit 118.
It already appears to be coming down rapidly.
I was honestly expecting this to be a SHTF situation. However, it appears I was wrong.
ITT, we state our opinions and argue whether it is so over or we are so back.

Early replies (first 3)

61958990
>itt brown third worldists cope about Iran being a leaderless paper tiger.
You honestly thought 68 iq goat rapists dictate our fucking energy policy?
You all bought at the top, again. Lol who am I kidding none of you have money let alone investments.
Kys

61959005
>>61958990
>OP: "I was honestly expecting this to be a SHTF situation. However, it appears I was wrong."
>Anon: YOU ARE A SHITSKIN WHO BOUGHT THE TOP!
>This anon doesn't know how to read.

61959012
>>61959005
You are brown!!!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61959033
>>61959012
Blah blah blah, nigger nigger nigger.
Now, denounce the Talmud and type the word "GOD" in your next post to prove you are not Jewish.

61959060
>>61958974
econ expert here
prices could go up or they could go down

61959073
>>61959060
TRVKE!
HOLY SHIT THIS ANON IS CORRECT!

61959099
>>61959033
God knows you are brown. Now never post on the Internet again.

61959113
>>61959099
He said God.
I noticed that you refused to deny the Talmud.
Curious...
>HE SUPPORTS THE SIDE THAT BURNED A DEMON! HE MUST BE BROWN!!!

61959134
>>61959113
Jews are brown, just like you, retard.

61959150
>>61959134
Continuously neglects to deny the Talmud.
Why are you so butthurt, anon?
I merely wanted to discuss the impact of crude futures on the U.S. and the world at large.

61959154
>>61958974
The rest of the brown world will be more negatively impacted than the USA and we (the USA) will continue accumulating the worlds wealth while you (brown) will seethe

61959161
>>61959154
HE DOESN'T SUPPORT (((CURRENT THING)))!!!
HE MUST BE BROWN! GET HIM!

61959163
>>61959150
Brother I hate jews because they are jews. You hate jews because you think they are white. And thats because you are brown.
>denounce the Talmud
Okay I denounce it.
Now you denounce Mohammed.

61959169
>>61959161
You are so brown.

61959188
>>61959163
This post puts the HAM in MoHAMmed!
Mohammed is a pedo who married a 3-year-old (Aisha).

61959211
>>61959188
You may not be a Muslim but you are still certainly brown and you think jews are white, which is extremely stupid.
So essentially you a low iq brown that will never accumulate wealth cause you do brown behavior. Like siding with 68 iq goat rapists

61959223
>>61959211
>I think Kikes are white.
I'll take shit I never said for 2500.
